# portkey_users (Data Source)

Fetches all Portkey users in the organization. Automatically paginates through all pages. Supports optional server-side filtering by email or role.

## Example Usage

### Fetch all users

```terraform
data "portkey_users" "all" {}

output "total_users" {
  value = length(data.portkey_users.all.users)
}
```

### Look up a user by email

```terraform
data "portkey_users" "manager" {
  email = "manager@example.com"
}

resource "portkey_workspace_member" "manager" {
  workspace_id = portkey_workspace.example.id
  user_id      = data.portkey_users.manager.users[0].id
  role         = "manager"
}
```

### Filter by role

```terraform
data "portkey_users" "admins" {
  role = "admin"
}

output "admin_emails" {
  value = [for u in data.portkey_users.admins.users : u.email]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `email` (String) Filter users by email address. When set, only users matching this email are returned.
- `role` (String) Filter users by organization role. Valid values: `admin`, `member`, `owner`.

### Read-Only

- `users` (Attributes List) List of users. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--users))

<a id="nestedatt--users"></a>
### Nested Schema for `users`

Read-Only:

- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the user was created.
- `email` (String) Email address of the user.
- `id` (String) User identifier.
- `role` (String) Organization role of the user.
- `status` (String) Status of the user account.
- `updated_at` (String) Timestamp when the user was last updated.
